chunk_summary: This section outlines a standardized outpatient dietary assessment for macronutrient decisions in children, focusing on growth faltering or obesity. It emphasizes reproducible diet history, actionable portion measures, and energy displacement checks to guide interventions.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of reproducible diet history in macronutrient prescriptions for children.
Learn how to conduct a quick dietary assessment using a two-day recall and beverage/snack audits.
Identify energy displacement patterns and their impact on growth and nutrition.
Apply portion estimation using Indian household measures for actionable dietary advice.
key_facts: A two-day recall includes one typical weekday and one weekend/holiday day.
Portion estimation uses Indian measures: one katori (dal/curd/khichdi), roti size by palm diameter, one ladle (sabzi), and one cup (200–250 ml for milk/chaas).
Energy displacement often occurs due to excess milk, sweetened beverages, and frequent biscuits.
Growth faltering with adequate intake may indicate increased needs or malabsorption, requiring medical evaluation.
Short trials for abdominal symptoms should avoid prolonged unnecessary dietary restrictions.
reference_quote: A reproducible diet history is the bridge between a growth chart and a macronutrient prescription. (NICE NG75)
